content: To prevent brain fog and energy crashes, eating carbohydrates in the evening is recommended instead of midday.
Eating carbohydrates in the evening allows for a gradual rise and fall in blood sugar, which is beneficial for brain function and energy levels.
Consuming carbohydrates around 5 or 6 p.m. helps condition the body to run on fats for fuel in the morning, reducing the risk of midday energy crashes.
Carbohydrates consumed in the evening increase tryptophan uptake by the brain, leading to higher levels of serotonin and improved sleep quality.
A recommended carbohydrate intake before bed is approximately 50 grams for a 200-pound individual, providing a gentle rise and fall in blood sugar during sleep.
